note: Submodule of varnish_purger (project varnish_purge). Purges image-style derivatives from Varnish on entity update via URIBAN requests. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded), probing documented facts; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: What does the Varnish Image Purge submodule do when a content entity is updated?
evals.expected_output: On entity update it bans each image-style derivative of the entity's image fields in Varnish by sending URIBAN HTTP requests per derivative URL.

evals.prompt: Where is the Varnish Image Purge configuration form and what do you set there?
evals.expected_output: At /admin/config/development/varnish_image_purge (route varnish_image_purge.configuration) you tick which entity-type bundles trigger image purging.

evals.prompt: In Varnish Image Purge, what happens if I don't select any entity bundles in the config form?
evals.expected_output: If nothing is selected, all bundles of all content entity types are purged (only image-type fields).

evals.prompt: Which Varnish request method must my VCL handle for Varnish Image Purge to work?
evals.expected_output: URIBAN — your VCL must handle the URIBAN method (ban on host + url and return a synthetic 200), per the module's example snippet.
